label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's take a look at this question. The passage is about folk medicine, and we need to find out which of the given statements is true based on the information provided. First, I'll go through each option one by one and check against the passage. Option A: "It keeps one healthy by using medicines taken from plants and herbs." The first sentence of the passage says, "Folk medicine keeps the body healthy by using medicines taken from surrounding plants and herbs." That seems to match exactly. So A looks correct, but let's check the others just to be sure. Option B: "It can treat most kinds of diseases for both man and animals." The passage mentions that folk medicine was used by both man and animal, and that animals use plants to keep healthy. However, the passage doesn't say it can treat "most kinds of diseases." It's possible that this is an overstatement. The passage does say that materials for making medicine can be found everywhere, but it doesn't claim it's effective for most diseases. So B might not be correct. Option C: "It is better than other medicines in curing serious diseases." The passage doesn't compare folk medicine to other types of medicine or say it's better for serious diseases. In fact, it mentions that some beliefs around folk medicine are just old wives' tales and fairy tales. There's no indication that folk medicine is more effective than other types of medicine, so C is probably incorrect. Option D: "It was a way of body treatment used only in man's early history." The passage begins by saying that the use of folk medicine goes back to the time of earliest man, but it also says that one can practice folk medicine in any place of the world.
